title: Referential Integrity Management for DB2
text: Referential Integrity Management for DB2 DB2 allows referential integrity constraints to be defined only on the primary key of a parent table (the one entity). Uniface allows relationships to be defined on both primary and candidate keys. DB2 does not support referential integrity on candidate keys, so Uniface must maintain the constraints if your application has any relationships defined on candidate keys. Note: Constraint names are limited to 18 bytes. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
